channel.title: Introductions (new posts)
channel.description: Posts in the forum category "Introductions" - New to the site? Introduce yourself and meet other site members here.

item.title: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
item.content:encoded:
Hello hello Gruvint8! Welcome to the Wiki! I'm Ralliston, a site author and a member of the Community Outreach Team - with this helpful little comment, I'll try to help you find your way around the site and link a bunch of useful places and links that will help you in your SCP journey.

It's awesome to see someone with prior writing experience joining the wiki - I'm sure what you'll come up with during your stay here will be absolutely amazing. I can't wait to see your stuff. It's also great to see that after all of these years "lurking", you finally decided to make an account on-site. You'd be surprised at how many people stay only as lurkers and never properly become members. After all, now you can actively affect the quality of articles posted on-site by voting, you can comment, and, most importantly it seems, contribute.

Without further ado though, let's click this collapsible, shall we? :)

+ show block
– hide block

If you're going to try your hand at writing, try out our Sandbox. You can make yourself a page here to store your drafts, which are in-works articles. Using it, you can link the drafts to reviewers to get their opinions on them before posting. It really helps everyone out a lot.

For brainstorming concepts, try out the Ideas and Brainstorming forum. You can run your ideas by experienced members of the community if you need help refining and fleshing out your concept. It's a very helpful little place for when you want to start your writing journey, especially when you're not sure if the idea is all that great.

If you have a draft ready, make a thread in the Drafts and Critiques forum. Remember to check the stickied posts in the relevant thread for guidelines on how best to use each forum. You can also try the IRC chat for real-time feedback.

From site usage to writing tips, the Guide Hub contains a wealth of resources geared towards helping members produce successful articles. Within the Guides, you will find useful information written by experienced members of the site for the use of newer ones.

The more time you take to familiarize yourself with the SCP style, the drafting process, and the community, the greater your chances are for success! Remember - there is no rush to writing - take your time! The more time you spend on fleshing your ideas out, the more your chance of success becomes!

As far as reading is concerned, you might want to check out Top Rated New Pages, which will display the most popular recent articles. It's helpful to keep up with what's being received well by the community to implement into your very own writing. Alternatively, you might want to look at the User Curated Lists page, where members of the community list and rank their favorite articles in different genres and themes, showing which attempts at such are successful and what the community likes.

Best of luck with your future contributions to the site! I hope you enjoy your time here as much as we do! If you have any more questions, feel free to message me here on Wikidot or on the IRC chats!

item.title: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
item.content:encoded:
Good day everyone,

I'm a long time fan of SCP and have read my fair share of articles through the years. But since the start of corona I started getting into writing. I had allot of SCP ideas but was never sure about sharing them. Then in this past week, an idea of an SCP came to mind and I wrote it down. And after letting my close friends read it I was getting more comfortable with sharing my writing and finally decided to join. I hope to get better at writing through here and to just have an amazing time in this lovely community!

Regards,
TurtwigTheSpy (Some random Dutch Lad)

item.title: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
item.content:encoded:
Hi everybody, my names Phil and I'm just here chasing my joy.

I'm a recent University graduate who after some self evaluating recognized that the aspect of my degree I enjoyed most was the storytelling. I've always been a fan of the SCP universe and the way its many stories are spun together into an ever expanding universe of many minds. So I thought, where better to practice writing than here?

I'm a massive fan of collaborative story telling, a result of my immersion in ttrpg culture and nerdom, and really wanted the experience of discussing narrative with a community. This will be some of my first writing uploaded to a large public space and I find myself both excited and nervous to take the plunge. Thanks so much for anytime you might've spent on my ramblings and I hope to talk to and share my work with people soon. :)

item.title: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
item.content:encoded:
Man it's been a while since I've written any kind of introduction post.
Hi- I'm Leah. I originally just joined this Wiki because I wanted to read the SCP-4231 article and it is (rightfully so) hidden behind content warnings. But I've had a character idea for a while and so I started working on a tale surrounding her not long ago. Hopefully I'll become happy enough with it that I'll risk baring my creative soul to a bunch of strangers on a forum, which is an absolutely terrifying concept and I mean that with no sarcasm because I'm an anxious mess :)

item.title: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
item.content:encoded:
Greetings everyone! I am a beginner at writing so I decided to write SCP because:
1- I love the SCP lore
2- I have an ambition to be a renown writer
Anyway i look foward on working with you guys and knowing you all! I will already start working!

P.S- I'm Brazilian. Come to brazil please?

item.title: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
item.content:encoded:
Hey, I’m Zane, and I’ve been reading for several years, but only recently realized I could join the site. I don’t know what else to put here, except I want to write about a diner that if you eat the cheesecake, you get sent to another dimension. I also want to try to get into the 914 test logs. See you on the other side,
-Z

item.title: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
item.content:encoded:
Hi, this is my introduction as a hopeful writer of future SCPs and tales. I have been writing for a long time, and have been a follower of the SCP foundation for a long time, although I don't have an exact length of time. I'm not entirely sure what more information I should put into the intro, so here goes :)

Gruvint8

item.title: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Readers & Reviewers: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Readers & Reviewers
item.content:encoded:
Hi,

I’m Manseaweed, been following SCP stuff for quite awhile now and thought I should go ahead and make an account.

As for food, I’ve enjoyed some good quality ramen with friends.

item.title: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
item.content:encoded: Greeting everyone my name is classified of course but PLEASE call me tukapee or tuk for short, overtime I may give out my real name, unless thats a requirement, anyway the best food to eat with friends are berries! Cause their juicy, healthy, and full of flavor! I hope I can be agreat member to the community and have my scp on the list with the othera^^

item.title: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
item.content:encoded: Hey all, I've been reading up on a ton of SCPs recently, been aware of the community for a few years now. Figured I'd try my hand at writing one. If it goes well, I might try writing more in the near future if inspiration strikes me. I'm still waiting to join the sandbox wiki, but if anyone can point me in the direction here on the main site where I can get feedback on the SCP I'm writing I would greatly appreciate it.

item.title: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Readers & Reviewers: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Readers & Reviewers
item.content:encoded:
Hi! Nightshade210 here. I've been lurking on the site for a while, but finally built up the courage to actually join the forums. I really like writing and brainstorming concepts, but I'm pretty sure it'll take a while until I post any writing here (if I ever do, you know, finish any of them). English isn't my first language, so if anything I write is confusing in any measure, please let me know! I'll appreciate it!

Best food to share is pizza. Period.

item.title: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Readers & Reviewers: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Readers & Reviewers
item.content:encoded:
'mornin fellas, call me Zetros. I've known about the wiki for a few years now, but only read a little bit back then, but decided to start reading once again after watching the Overlord short film (which was very good!). I'm probably gonna just reserve myself to reading, although I wouldn't mind dabbling in writing an SCP someday. Maybe not. Who knows. That being said I'm pretty interested in cars, I spend most of my time (and money!) turning wrenches and attempting to do janky solutions then finding out that maybe using zipties to fix something isn't quite the best way to do it :P

That being said, if any one of y'all writers have any questions regarding automobile function in a tale or SCP page, feel free to ask me for a hand. I'm sure I can lend a hand :)

Best food to eat with friends is chain pizza. Can't be the fancy stuff, only Dominoes or co. on a whim at 1am.

item.title: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Readers & Reviewers: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Readers & Reviewers
item.content:encoded:
Hello World! My username is DeadRainMan. I found the site after viewing several YouTube videos about the foundation. Comically it took me quite a while to realize these writings are all fiction.

What my main goal with joining the website is to fix spelling and grammatical errors in articles i read on the site, but I will probably spend most of my time reading the stories and asking questions in the forums.

To answer the random question, no food is better than Italian spaghetti.

item.title: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
item.content:encoded:
Hello!

I really enjoy writing, and decided to try out writing my own SCP. It's going to take some work, as I want my first SCP to not fail (my idea is centered around an anomalous nuclear power plant of unknown origin in northen Quebec). I also decided to drop in here, as it seems like it's rude to pop in to a community and not get to know anybody.

I mean, looking at the other forums, everybody seems pretty nice, so that makes me want to be a part of that community.

Anyways, not sure how I came off there. Oh well.

item.title: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
item.content:encoded:
Hello hello DrTarin! Welcome to the Wiki! I'm Ralliston, a site author and a member of the Community Outreach Team - with this helpful little comment, I'll try to help you find your way around the site and link a bunch of useful places and links that will help you in your SCP journey.

It's great to see that after all of these years, you finally decided to make an account on-site. After all, that's the only way you can contribute - speaking of which, it's also awesome to see you want to be a site author too! Don't worry, I'm sure that during your stay here you definitely will gain some knowledge regarding how to write things the site likes and how to write stuff you are happy with, too!

Without further ado though, let's click this collapsible, shall we? :)

+ show block
– hide block

If you're going to try your hand at writing, try out our Sandbox. You can make yourself a page here to store your drafts, which are in-works articles. Using it, you can link the drafts to reviewers to get their opinions on them before posting. It really helps everyone out a lot.

For brainstorming concepts, try out the Ideas and Brainstorming forum. You can run your ideas by experienced members of the community if you need help refining and fleshing out your concept. It's a very helpful little place for when you want to start your writing journey, especially when you're not sure if the idea is all that great.

If you have a draft ready, make a thread in the Drafts and Critiques forum. Remember to check the stickied posts in the relevant thread for guidelines on how best to use each forum. You can also try the IRC chat for real-time feedback.

From site usage to writing tips, the Guide Hub contains a wealth of resources geared towards helping members produce successful articles. Within the Guides, you will find useful information written by experienced members of the site for the use of newer ones.

The more time you take to familiarize yourself with the SCP style, the drafting process, and the community, the greater your chances are for success! Remember - there is no rush to writing - take your time! The more time you spend on fleshing your ideas out, the more your chance of success becomes!

As far as reading is concerned, you might want to check out Top Rated New Pages, which will display the most popular recent articles. It's helpful to keep up with what's being received well by the community to implement into your very own writing. Alternatively, you might want to look at the User Curated Lists page, where members of the community list and rank their favorite articles in different genres and themes, showing which attempts at such are successful and what the community likes.

Best of luck with your future contributions to the site! I hope you enjoy your time here as much as we do! If you have any more questions, feel free to message me here on Wikidot or on the IRC chats!

item.title: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
item.content:encoded:
Hello everyone!

I´ve had my head wrapped around SCPs a year ago, and I always wanted to write my own SCPs. Although I am not a pro at writing I still love to create SCPs. I don't mind any criticism as long as it helps me to improve on my writing.

item.title: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
item.content:encoded:
Hello there, my name is Angelo, it is a pleasure to be here.

I'm quite an old SCP fan, but only now I've decided to try and get closer to its fans and, in the near future, I will also take a crack at writing my own anomaly. I hope we can get along just fine!

About this month's question, I find it nice to have ice cream and, maybe, pizza with other people.

item.title: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Readers & Reviewers: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Readers & Reviewers
item.content:encoded:
Hello, my name is Cecil. I've been lurking on the wiki anonymously for quite a while now, ever since two years ago, Volgun posted that video about SCP-1504 and it snuck into my recommendations. I enjoy drawing and long lists. Also classifications, they're neat. I think I'll continue just reading for now, I don't think I have enough knowledge to critique or write yet. I plan on doing that in the future, though.

Ice cream, honestly. Infinite tastes, infinite options. Gives you a feeling of 343-like power. Also, everyone can find a taste that suits them or something I guess

item.title: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
item.content:encoded:
Hello hello baydog! Welcome to the Wiki! I'm Ralliston, a site author and a member of the Community Outreach Team - with this helpful little comment, I'll try to help you find your way around the site and link a bunch of useful places and links that will help you in your SCP journey.

It's great to see that after all of these years, you finally decided to make an account on-site. You'd be surprised at how many people remain lurkers forever - it's a real shame, considering that becoming ~one of us~ can not only let you vote, which also makes you able to affect the quality of works posted onsite, but also lets you comment and contribute. Speaking of which - it's great to see you want to do that. I can't wait to see what you'll come up with!

Without further ado though, let's click this collapsible, shall we? :)

+ show block
– hide block

If you're going to try your hand at writing, try out our Sandbox. You can make yourself a page here to store your drafts, which are in-works articles. Using it, you can link the drafts to reviewers to get their opinions on them before posting. It really helps everyone out a lot.

For brainstorming concepts, try out the Ideas and Brainstorming forum. You can run your ideas by experienced members of the community if you need help refining and fleshing out your concept. It's a very helpful little place for when you want to start your writing journey, especially when you're not sure if the idea is all that great.

If you have a draft ready, make a thread in the Drafts and Critiques forum. Remember to check the stickied posts in the relevant thread for guidelines on how best to use each forum. You can also try the IRC chat for real-time feedback.

From site usage to writing tips, the Guide Hub contains a wealth of resources geared towards helping members produce successful articles. Within the Guides, you will find useful information written by experienced members of the site for the use of newer ones.

The more time you take to familiarize yourself with the SCP style, the drafting process, and the community, the greater your chances are for success! Remember - there is no rush to writing - take your time! The more time you spend on fleshing your ideas out, the more your chance of success becomes!

As far as reading is concerned, you might want to check out Top Rated New Pages, which will display the most popular recent articles. It's helpful to keep up with what's being received well by the community to implement into your very own writing. Alternatively, you might want to look at the User Curated Lists page, where members of the community list and rank their favorite articles in different genres and themes, showing which attempts at such are successful and what the community likes.

Best of luck with your future contributions to the site! I hope you enjoy your time here as much as we do! If you have any more questions, feel free to message me here on Wikidot or on the IRC chats!

item.title: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Readers & Reviewers: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Readers & Reviewers
item.content:encoded:
Hi! I'm Eltrafix

I've been reading on the SCP wiki for a few years and finally decided to join. I've written in the past and I'm hoping to write some SCP's in the future. I'll stay a reader for the time being though.

Food to share: Sushi, hotpot or tabletop grilling - wholesome and fun until the food runs out and mockfights ensue :P
